next_step_text: 6.29.26 KK - had commercial call. Pricing well received. They understand the timeline is tight and they need to make a decision in the coming week or so. RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday. sharing commercials with value slides on why zycus for platform and partnership offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash 6/22mc- Followed up with Bass Pro to re-engage David and Whitney on the updated pr

recent_calls_14d.subject: Proposal Review with Zycus - Bass Pro

our_open_deliverables.text: Deliver structured change-management plan (workshops, lunch-and-learns, on-site open office sessions) as contractual deliverable to de-risk 2,000-user adoption
Conduct alignment session with AP team to address their concerns about transition and build internal sponsorship
Deliver 2-3 customer references from prior Coupa P2P replacements, specifically addressing transition experience, user adoption, and change-management approach
Send Bass Pro the link to last year's Horizon event recordings so they can preview Zycus customer community and product roadmap content
Deliver commercials with value slides on why Zycus for platform and partnership (in progress, sent 29 June per Next Step)

Send updated commercial proposal to Bass Pro reflecting 5-year contract term (vs 3-year), revised spend assumptions ($250M P2P vs $1.04B total), 1,500 procurement/intake users (vs 2,000), and implementation cost detail with 4-6 milestone payments amortized over 12-18 month timeline

Update and resend the commercial proposal to Bass Pro to include the updated Emburse T&E solution details, along with the meeting deck, call recording, and a closing plan outlining implementation milestones

buyer_dependent_items.text: Schedule deep-dive session on iSource, iContract, iSupplier modules after internal socialization period (stated as 'couple weeks'), including AP team, Corey's replacement, and category managers
Review updated commercial proposal and confirm scope/pricing alignment
Confirm revised commercial assumptions (spend, user counts, contract term) and return data validation to Zycus so updated proposal accurately reflects Bass Pro's environment
Facilitate AP team alignment session to address concerns and build internal sponsorship for Zycus evaluation
Facilitate intro to CFO and executive sponsor (the deal owner's manager, David's boss the CPO) for commercial sign-off discussion
Make decision in coming week or so (stated 29 June; no next meeting scheduled as of 29 June Next Step)

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the incumbent of 6 years and the live primary threat — it is actively defending with an on-site visit this week (per 29 Jun Next Step), and 'renew Coupa / do nothing' is the realistic alternative since David admits Coupa 'can serve our business' and the only real obstacle is change management. The single strongest current threat is the incumbent's inertia advantage combined with its renewed on-site push landing BEFORE Zycus has delivered its overdue adoption-proof references. Zip was floated once as an intake option but never adopted and is dormant.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business or you can serve our business. The issue is change management.
competitors.how_we_win: De-risk change management with Coupa-replacement references and a contractual adoption plan delivered before the on-site; lean on single-code-base agentic Merlin + Teams-native guided buying vs Coupa's acquisition-stitched, degraded analytics and slow PE-era support

competitors.name: Coupa (incumbent)
competitors.quote: Ticket support response times from Coupa are slow, impacting error resolution. Coupa's spend analysis used to be great pre-2021 but recent update degraded functionality. CFO challenged procurement to evaluate market alternatives beyond the current suite.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 12 March 2026 (Whitney) + demo 30 March 2026 (David)
competitors.how_we_win: Prove low-friction Coupa replacement at 2,000-user scale via customer references (David's explicit ask 27 May), deliver unified intake David stated they lack (30 March), and tie the decision to the ~Jan 2027 Coupa cutover to keep urgency alive. Wedge: Teams-native guided buying eliminates the learning curve David fears, Merlin intake unifies fragmented channels, AI taxonomy fixes the analytics complexity Whitney flagged.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Renew Coupa
competitors.quote: RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday
competitors.how_we_win: Keep urgency alive around the ~Jan/Feb 2027 renewal and ~6-month implementation runway; quantify the cost of inertia (degraded analytics, slow support, fragmented intake); deliver the adoption-proof references so 'staying put' is no longer the lower-risk option

competitors.name: Others
competitors.quote: Listed in Salesforce Competitors__c field but not named or discussed on any call.
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown competitor, likely a data artifact, no active competitive threat from this source.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: we looked at Zip... it looks great. But unless it ties into your other things, then it doesn't have a lot of benefit.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as integrated end-to-end vs standalone intake point tools

champion_strength.source: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026; SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026; commercial call 29 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: David Elford is a developing champion driving the evaluation and declaring full-suite intent, but has gated executive access and not yet introduced the CFO/CPO or the AP team. The conversion play is to hand him the references and a contractual change-management plan as the artifacts he carries upward — turning a gatekeeper into a sponsor. Whitney Parks supports actively.

sources: Proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford: internal sign-off required)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun and 29 Jun 2026 (planned CFO/CPO escalation)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is not engaged, and access is champion-gated. David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is driving the evaluation but is explicitly not the final approver: on 6 Apr he said the proposal must be socialized with 'a bunch of other internal folks that would need to sign off before we moved at all,' and he has insisted the process move under his direction. Budget authority sits above him with the CFO (who mandated the evaluation) and the CPO/his boss, but neither has been met. Because David has gated executive access, the path to power runs THROUGH him — the move is to arm David to broker a CFO/CPO alignment session, not to cold-outreach the EB and burn his goodwill.

recommended_moves: Deliver the 2-3 Coupa-replacement reference calls with prepared questions to David this week — ahead of Coupa's on-site.
Co-author a contractual change-management plan (workshops, on-site go-live support, adoption KPIs) as an SOW Schedule A and walk David through it.
Arm David to broker a CFO/CPO executive alignment session — hand him the references, the change-management plan, and a one-page value case rather than cold-outreaching the EB.
